features.summary: Caterpillar reported a strong second quarter for 2023, with sales and revenues increasing by 22% to $17.3 billion, driven by higher sales volume and price realization. The adjusted operating profit margin improved to 21.3%, and the company generated $2.6 billion in ME&T free cash flow. The backlog at the end of the quarter stood at $30.7 billion, reflecting healthy demand across most end markets.
features.management_outlook: Management expects full-year 2023 results to exceed previous forecasts, anticipating adjusted operating profit margins to be near the top of the targeted range and ME&T free cash flow to be around the top of the $4 billion to $8 billion range. They foresee continued growth in non-residential construction in North America and healthy mining demand, although they acknowledge potential challenges in Europe and China.
features.risks: Caterpillar faces risks related to global macroeconomic conditions, supply chain challenges, and potential fluctuations in dealer inventory levels. The company is monitoring these factors closely, particularly in relation to large engines and certain machines that are still experiencing supply constraints.
features.key_drivers: Key drivers of Caterpillar's performance include strong demand in the Energy & Transportation segment, particularly for oil and gas applications, and robust sales in Resource Industries supported by high commodity prices. Additionally, improvements in dealer inventory levels and production capabilities have contributed positively to sales growth.
